entries.title: Here’s how to ask Gemini Live for help with anything you see.
entries.summary: Have you ever struggled to describe something you’re looking at? Whether it’s a complex manual, a blinking error code, or a unique object, sometimes you just need an exp…
entries.content:
Have you ever struggled to describe something you’re looking at? Whether it’s a complex manual, a blinking error code, or a unique object, sometimes you just need an expert to see what you see. With the Gemini app, you can skip the long descriptions and capture exactly what’s in front of you.

Simply tap the Live icon and click the camera to start Gemini Live, and point your lens at whatever you need help with. Ask Gemini for step-by-step repair instructions or tips for organizing messy drawers. Or share your screen to get assistance navigating a confusing app.

You can even get creative, using Gemini to brainstorm decor ideas or give you personalized advice as you shop. Get real-time help for anything you can think of, using the real world as your starting point.

entries.title: Switch to Android easily — and bring your data with you.
entries.summary: A new migration experience built directly into Android 17 that lets you transfer more data wirelessly from an iPhone.
entries.content:
Switching to Android just got even easier. With the launch of Samsung’s new Z Series at today’s Galaxy Unpacked, we introduced a new native migration experience built directly into Android 17 that lets you transfer even more data types from an iPhone wirelessly — without downloading a separate app.

With the upgraded method, you can easily bring over what matters most. This includes your photos, videos, contacts, messages, and calendar — plus, newly supported data, such as your Google Account, passwords, Wi-Fi credentials, and even your eSIM.

This upgrade has already started rolling out to select Pixel devices. It will also be available on the new Samsung Galaxy Z Flip8 and Z Fold8 series devices and come to more Android devices soon.

Visit android.com/switch for more switching to Android tips.

entries.title: 3 Google updates from Galaxy Unpacked 2026
entries.summary: Gentle Monster glasses, Warby Parker glasses, a prompt asking for the history behind a pictured building, and a prompt asking to book a table at a pictured restaurant
entries.content:
Today at Galaxy Unpacked in London, we celebrated the launch of the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra, Fold8, and Flip8. With these devices, we are making it easier than ever to get started on Android while also rolling out the first capabilities of Gemini Intelligence 1 — our vision for technology that works proactively for you. Whether you choose to unfold your phone to maximize your productivity on a bigger screen or keep it folded so you can focus on your day, Gemini Intelligence is there to help with the heavy lifting.

Here is a look at three updates coming to the newest devices.

1. Get next-generation task automation with Gemini Intelligence.

Gemini Intelligence can help you offload digital chores so you can spend less time behind a screen. Back in February, we introduced a beta for task automation, helping you delegate simple everyday tasks to Gemini, such as ordering a rideshare or getting your favorite takeout meal for delivery.

Now task automation is expanding support from a handful of apps to more than 40 popular apps. 2 This means Gemini can now help you handle more of your daily "life admin" — from shopping and making dinner reservations to booking travel experiences and buying event tickets. On the Galaxy Z Flip8, you can even access this assistance directly from the Flex Window by long pressing the power button.

Plus, Gemini can now use advanced reasoning to understand what is on your screen and parse complex images as prompts. This gives you a powerful jumpstart — because sometimes, the easiest way to explain what you need is to show Gemini a shopping list or inspiration photo.

While Gemini works to complete the task, you can track the step-by-step progress on your screen, multitask in other apps, or close your phone and let Gemini run in the background. If you need to make a quick adjustment or add a personal touch, you can step in to handle a specific detail and then hand control back to Gemini to complete the rest. Gemini is designed to notify you when the task is ready for your review and final confirmation.

2. Raise the bar on your projects with Gemini Notebook.

To help you manage deep projects and synthesize complex information, we are bringing the power of Gemini Notebook (formerly NotebookLM) directly to your device. The app comes preinstalled on Samsung's new foldables. 3

Gemini Notebook is your thinking and research partner powered by our advanced multimodal models. On the Galaxy Z Fold8 series' expansive display, you can drag and drop photos, documents, whiteboard images, voice recordings, and more directly into a side-by-side workspace.

You can use the chat function in Gemini Notebook to ask questions about your sources or go one step further and create custom, high-quality resources — including slide decks, videos, flash cards, quizzes, or even a podcast — tailored to how you learn best.

To help you tackle your most ambitious projects, we are including a six-month trial of Google AI Pro on every Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra, Fold8, and Flip8, which gives you more access to powerful features, such as Audio Overviews, Video Overviews, Slides, and more.

3. Take Gemini on the go with watches and glasses.

Our collaboration with Samsung extends beyond smartphones. On the Galaxy Watch 9, you have more convenient access to Gemini on the go. Simply raise your wrist and Gemini will activate so you can ask it questions, no wake word required.

We’re also bringing Gemini to glasses. At Google I/O 2026, we announced intelligent eyewear — created in partnership with Samsung and launching this fall — and shared two designs from our partners, eyewear brands Gentle Monster and Warby Parker. Today we revealed two more frames and shared more about how Gemini’s helpfulness will come to life on these new devices. For example if you’re wearing a compatible Wear OS watch, you’ll be able to control your glasses with just a hand gesture.

Whether you are looking to simplify your daily routine or boost your productivity, today's announcements are just the beginning of what we have in store. We will roll out more intelligent features as they are ready, so you’re not left waiting.

entries.title: 13 Google tips for a fun, productive summer off from college
entries.summary: Illustration of a woman in front of a computer, a phone searching an image of a plant, a suitcase, and a video feed
entries.content:
It's summer in the Northern Hemisphere. That means it’s prime time for beach trips, backyard barbecues, and afternoons at the pool. And if you’re a college student, it’s also the perfect season for getting ahead of your to-do list.

Need to find a fall internship, study for admissions exams, master a new skill, or prep for the school year ahead? Here are 13 ways Google’s AI tools can help you get things done with less stress — so you can spend more time enjoying your summer.

Apply for a fall internship.

1. Not sure where to start your internship search? Brainstorm options with Career Dreamer, an AI-powered tool that helps you explore possible job opportunities. Input your experiences, skills, and interests, and find potential career paths tailored to your background.

2. Build a dashboard to track internship opportunities — and application deadlines — using Canvas in AI Mode. Simply open AI Mode in Search and turn on the Canvas tool to open a dedicated workspace. Then try a conversational prompt, like: “Help me build a dashboard to track fall internships in NYC focused on marketing and communications in fashion. Include details on deadlines and application requirements.”

3. Workshop your resume and cover letter with Gemini Notebook, an AI-powered research partner. Create a notebook for your internship search. Then upload all your career clutter — old projects, positive feedback, resumes, and more. Gemini Notebook can help you fine-tune your application materials, identify key strengths, and turn your background into a coherent narrative to pitch to employers.

4. Prep for upcoming interviews by rehearsing with Gemini Live. You can use this feature to have a natural, free-flowing conversation out loud — just like you would in an interview. Practice answering common interview questions, and ask Gemini for real-time feedback on your delivery, pacing, and tone. It’s like having an interview coach in your pocket.

Try new things.

5. Explore a hobby, pick up a creative project, or master a new skill with YouTube Learning. YouTube’s educational library is packed with top-tier instructional content. And on select videos, you’ll see an “Ask” button that lets you dive even deeper. Tap it to find out what a video covers, generate a summary of its key points, or even create a brief quiz on its contents — all without leaving the video.

6. When you need help picking up a new skill — like crafting the perfect cup of matcha — consider Search Live your very own expert adviser. Just open the Google app (Android and iOS) and tap the Live icon under the search bar. Then point your camera at the equipment in your matcha set and ask what each tool is used for. You can also get quick advice on which ingredients you can substitute if you’re looking for low-sugar or dairy-free alternatives for a matcha latte.

7. Get instant feedback on your sports form with Gemini video analysis. Let’s say you’ve taken up pickleball over the summer. Record a quick clip of yourself hitting a few shots, and ask Gemini how you can improve. It will break down your paddle angle, stance, and follow-through, helping you understand what to adjust.

8. Use Lens in the Google app to dive deeper into your interests, whether you’re expanding your plant collection or exploring local history. Maybe you see a cool plant, but you’re not sure what it’s called or how to take care of it. Snap a photo with Lens, and add “how much sunlight does this need” to learn more. You can do the same with unique landmarks in your area. Take a picture with Lens and ask “what inspired this design?”

9. Maintain healthy digital habits with Android’s built-in screen time tools. When you’re in the zone of learning something new, it’s easy to lose track of time and stare at your screen for hours. So set take a break reminders or a bedtime schedule to keep your days balanced. That way, you can stay refreshed and ready for your summer internship, vacation, or whatever else the season has in store.

Study smarter.

10. Applying to grad school? Use study notebooks in Gemini to prep for standardized tests, like the GRE (available in Gemini later this summer). After assessing your knowledge gaps with a diagnostic quiz, Gemini will keep you on track with bite-sized lessons and progress checks.

11. AI Mode in Search can help you create custom study guides for summer courses, using the Canvas tool. Just select Canvas from the plus (+) menu and ask AI Mode something like “make a study guide for Impressionism, include key periods, and highlight the main artworks.” You’ll get a comprehensive study guide, complete with relevant links and images. Then, you can ask follow-up questions, upload your notes to add more context, and either share a link to the guide with your classmates or export it to Google Docs.

12. Turn your notes into a podcast with Gemini Notebook. Let’s say you’re planning to apply to law school this fall. Upload your LSAT study materials into a notebook. Then use the Audio Overview feature to create a podcast between two AI hosts discussing your notes. You can listen to your custom podcast while riding the subway or walking to your internship. And you can even interrupt the hosts with questions if you don’t understand something.

13. Organize last semester’s notes by creating a dedicated Gemini notebook for each class you took. Upload everything: whiteboard photos, lecture videos, problem sets, class notes — you name it. Gemini can transform your files into whatever format helps you learn best, like a study guide, practice quiz, infographic, or interactive visualization. And when you want to review your notes, try explaining the concept out loud using Gemini Live. It’ll spot the gaps in your knowledge, asking follow-up questions to make sure you still understand the material.

entries.title: Introducing Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and 3.5 Flash Cyber
entries.summary: a hero image saying 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and 3.5 Flash Cyber
entries.content:
Developers and customers building production AI agents need higher token efficiency, lower latency, and more reliable performance. Our Flash series of models is built to meet the sweet spot of efficiency and quality to enable scaling agentic workflows. Building on Gemini 3.5 Flash, we’re introducing new Gemini models:

  • 3.6 Flash: Our workhorse model that delivers better coding, knowledge work, and multimodal performance. According to the Artificial Analysis Index, it reduces output token usage by 17% compared to 3.5 Flash, and in some benchmarks like DeepSWE byDatacurve, we observe up to 65%, all at a lower cost per output token.
  • 3.5 Flash-Lite: Our fastest, most cost-effective 3.5-class model, delivering 350 output tokens per second according to the Artificial Analysis Index, also significantly outperforming prior Flash-Lite generations in agentic workflows.
  • 3.5 Flash Cyber in CodeMender: Successful cybersecurity applications require careful orchestration of a model alongside an agent infrastructure. We’re introducing a combination of a new, highly efficient, specialized cyber-focused model paired with our CodeMender code security agent that delivers competitive performance at the frontier.

Beyond today’s releases, Gemini 3.5 Pro is currently testing with partners and we plan to make it broadly available as soon as it’s ready. In parallel, our team is already focusing on building the next generation of models. We have started our most ambitious pre-training run yet, for Gemini 4, and are excited by the progress.

3.6 Flash: More efficient and better quality than 3.5 Flash

Gemini 3.6 Flash builds directly on developer and customer feedback from 3.5 Flash. 3.6 Flash not only delivers a step up in coding and knowledge work, but it does this while meaningfully improving token efficiency. For example, on the Artificial Analysis Index, we see 3.6 Flash consuming 17% fewer output tokens than 3.5 Flash. It also takes fewer reasoning steps and tool calls to accomplish multi-step workflows.

This enhanced efficiency is also combined with a lower price than 3.5 Flash. At $1.50/1M input tokens and $7.50/1M output tokens, 3.6 Flash reduces the overall cost per agentic task, making agents more cost-effective to build and run.

3.6 Flash shows better token efficiency and reduced verbosity than 3.5 Flash in an OSWorld verified task (API)

Even while being more efficient, 3.6 Flash sees performance gains compared to 3.5 Flash across use cases:

  • 3.6 Flash delivers higher precision with fewer unwanted code edits and reduced execution loops, as seen in DeepSWE (49% vs. 37%), and shows significant improvement in ML Research, as seen in MLE Bench (63.9% vs. 49.7%).
  • It has improved computer use capabilities as seen in OSWorld-Verified (83.0% vs. 78.4%). Computer use is now a built-in client side tool via the Gemini API and Gemini Enterprise.
  • It outperforms 3.5 Flash in knowledge work, as shown by benchmarks like GDPval-AA v2 (1421 vs. 1349). Customers like Hebbia and Harvey have found it particularly capable at multimodal tasks like document parsing, chart and data analysis, and report drafting.

Customers report 3.6 Flash is a step forward in both cost and quality, balancing token efficiency, accuracy, and speed across complex workflows and knowledge-based tasks:

Built with safety

3.6 Flash is shipping with enhanced Frontier Safety safeguards in the domains of Chemical, Biological, Radiological, and Nuclear (CBRN) and cyber offense misuses. These safeguards make the model substantially more resistant to jailbreaks. At the same time, the model has been trained to minimize refusals for beneficial uses.

For more information, see the 3.6 Flash model card.

3.5 Flash-Lite: Built to scale agentic workflows

Beyond Flash, we’re also releasing Gemini 3.5 Flash-Lite, designed for both low-latency tasks and tasks where high throughput is critical for developers workflows, like agentic search and document processing.

3.5 Flash-Lite is the fastest model in the 3.5 series. As measured by Artificial Analysis, it runs at 350 output tokens/s. Priced at $0.3/1M input tokens and $2.5/1M output tokens and with significantly better quality than 3.1 Flash-Lite, 3.5 Flash-Lite offers a strong price-to-performance ratio for developers and customers running high throughput production traffic.

3.5 Flash-Lite executes high volume tasks at a lower latency than 3.5 Flash.

3.5 Flash-Lite enables efficient scaling for agentic systems. Across thinking levels, the model significantly outperforms 3.1 Flash-Lite. Depending on the workload, developers can configure the model to prioritize low-latency, low-cost execution for high-volume tasks with the minimal and low thinking levels, or engage higher thinking levels to process multi-step subagent workloads. The model now also has computer use as a built-in tool to reliably support these agentic tasks across surfaces.

It’s a significant step up in coding and agentic tasks as seen in Terminal-Bench 2.1 (54% vs 31%), long context as seen in GDM-MRCR v2 (72.2% vs. 60.1%), and real-world task execution as seen in GDPval-AA v2 (1140 vs. 642).

In fact, on many agentic and coding evals, 3.5 Flash-Lite even outperforms 3 Flash, including on SWE-Bench Pro (54.2% vs. 49.6%) and OSWorld-Verified (74.0% vs. 65.1%), making it a faster & more capable option for workloads on both 2.5 and 3 Flash.

Early customers of 3.5 Flash-Lite are highlighting its unique combination of speed, intelligence, and cost efficiency for scaling agentic workflows and data processing tasks:

For more information about the model, see the 3.5 Flash-Lite model card.

3.5 Flash Cyber in CodeMender: finding and fixing vulnerabilities efficiently

AI models have become capable of finding security vulnerabilities faster than current systems can fix them. Tackling this growing threat requires an approach to securing software that is highly capable and efficient.

Flash’s performance and efficiency makes it an ideal foundation to detect, validate, and patch code security issues at scale. Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber is built on top of 3.5 Flash, and fine-tuned for finding and fixing cybersecurity vulnerabilities at a lower price per token than larger models.

Within CodeMender, which uses multiple 3.5 Flash Cyber agents working together to produce a single combined report, 3.5 Flash Cyber reaches competitive performance at the frontier on the popular benchmark CyberGym.

Given the dual-use nature of this technology, we have taken an intentional approach to deploying 3.5 Flash Cyber. The model will be exclusively available to governments and trusted partners via CodeMender soon as part of a limited-access pilot program. This will give frontline defenders a head start in finding and fixing critical vulnerabilities before they can be exploited, while mitigating against broader misuse.

3.6 Flash and 3.5 Flash-Lite: Get started today

3.6 Flash and 3.5 Flash-Lite are available starting today:

  • For developers in the Gemini API via Google AI StudioandAndroid Studio. 3.6 Flash is also available inGoogle Antigravity. Get started with theDeveloper Guide.
  • For enterprises in Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform. 3.6 Flash is also available in theGemini Enterprise app.
  • For everyone via the Gemini app. 3.5 Flash-Lite is also rolling out in Google Search.

As you start building with 3.6 Flash and 3.5 Flash-Lite, we welcome your feedback to improve future Gemini models and look forward to releasing 3.5 Pro soon.

entries.title: We’re announcing the Alliance for America’s Skilled Trades.
entries.summary: Google is joining BlackRock, Carhartt and Ford to launch the Alliance for America’s Skilled Trades.
entries.content:
To help meet the growing demand for skilled trades workers, Google is joining forces with BlackRock, Carhartt, and Ford to launch the Alliance for America’s Skilled Trades. The founding members of the Alliance have already independently committed to supporting skilled workforce training initiatives in 30 states across the U.S.

To multiply our impact, the alliance is focusing on three key areas:

  • Building a stronger pipeline for the skilled trades.
  • Scaling proven, evidence-based training approaches.
  • Inviting new partners to help expand opportunities nationwide.

For Google, this builds on our commitment to help train more than 300,000 American workers for in-demand, high-paying careers. By working together, we can ensure the economic benefits of new technologies are shared by everyone.

Learn more about the alliance in this op-ed co-authored by the founding companies.

entries.title: 5 ways to build a side hustle with Gemini
entries.summary: An illustration of a person sitting in a chair uploading files, and an AI sparkle icon
entries.content:
Searches for "start a business" hit an all-time high in the U.S. this year. Luckily, launching a business often no longer requires a massive budget or months of planning. With Gemini, you can turn an idea into income, automating complex tasks and kickstarting your growth from day one.

Here are five ways to make your passion project a profitable business:

1. Take your idea from a dream to a business plan.

Starting a business often begins with a single idea, but managing the messy explosion of thoughts that follows can be tough. Instead of scattering your notes across different places, brain dump your idea into a notebook in the Gemini app. Process your thoughts out loud, then add links, files, and other reference materials. Gemini will structure your vision, creating a central command center that grows alongside your business.

As your side hustle grows, graduate to business notebooks — a centralized hub to organize workflows and chats alongside your website and Google Business Profile. Grounded in your context, Gemini proactively surfaces critical action items, like unanswered customer questions, and recommends tailored updates to keep you ahead.

2. Research your market in minutes.

Gemini’s Deep Research feature compresses hours of work into minutes. (Think of it like a personal research assistant.) Ask it to generate a report on your competitors or markets you can target. It can browse hundreds of sources, track down facts and synthesize emerging trends, giving you professional analysis in a snap.

3. Define your brand aesthetic.

Use Gemini to build a standout brand on any budget. Drop your products into high-end backdrops, craft eye-catching typography, or make your own cinematic video ads. You’ll have consistent, professional-grade visuals for web pages and social posts in seconds.

Connect apps like Canva to use the assets you made in Gemini in creative layouts or social drafts without switching workspaces. You can also use Pomelli from Google Labs to build your core Business DNA, and use this to create a comprehensive brand book or stand up a complete website in just a few clicks.

4. Put your logistics on autopilot.

Drop your sales data, traffic logs, or social stats into Gemini to see what’s actually working. Ask it which products are driving profits, when customers are most active and where you can trim costs to save money.

Gemini Spark acts as your personal AI agent that runs in the background 24/7 — even when your laptop and phone are turned off. Instead of logging data manually, you can connect your tools and apps and let Spark handle the heavy lifting. For example, you can set an ongoing instruction, like: "Whenever I get an email asking about my services, automatically extract the client's details, store them in my 'Client Tracker' Google Sheet, and create a new dedicated folder for them in Google Drive."

Gemini Spark is available to Google AI Ultra subscribers globally.

5. Know when your price is right.

Pricing your services correctly is essential when you're getting ready to launch. Use Gemini to simplify the upfront math: Input your material costs, platform fees, and shipping expenses to find your break-even point, then ask it to model different pricing tiers to see how they impact your projected revenue.

Once your business is up and running, you can transform your decisions by grounding them in real data. Just upload your spreadsheets to Gemini to uncover hidden trends, generate personalized recommendations, and build a custom, interactive tool that helps you visualize the impact of potential decisions on your bottom line.

Ready to grow? Start your next project at gemini.google.com.

entries.title: Designing emoji for the way we communicate today
entries.summary: A series of emoji's: pancakes, tennis, sloth, fried egg, squidthumbs up
entries.content:
The way we use emoji has changed. In the early days, we were literal: You sent a nail polish emoji (💅) because you were, in fact, getting your nails polished.

Today we lean on emoji to break down the barrier of our screens and inject a shrug, a wink or that highly specific “it’s fine but it’s actually not fine” subtext. They’re the language within the language that keeps a blunt message from accidentally killing the vibe.

On World Emoji Day, here's a closer look at the evolution of our emoji (all 3,977 characters) and how they can add physical and emotional depth to how we communicate in the modern era.

Expression keeps evolving

We don't just "laugh" anymore. We collapse, or internally scream. Modern internet culture has steadily moved from mild expressions to drama, hyperbole and overwhelm.

For years, "face with tears of joy" (😂) was the undisputed emoji king, according to Gboard Federated Analytics. But following a multi-year decline, it slipped down the charts by 2025.

Why? Because 😭 is a masterclass in modern vocabulary: It’s hilarious, it’s devastating and it captures absolute overwhelm. When we do laugh, our inclination for hyperbole demands it be unhinged, pushing “rolling on the floor laughing” (🤣) to the top of the chart. Even our expressions for heartbreak are shifting; data show a poetic migration in popularity from the broken heart (💔) toward the wilted flower (🥀).

Art informs innovation

This update brings our favorite emoji into a 3D future rather than leaving them behind. The brain processes emoji faster than text, so you can’t just slap on a design and call it a day. Touching something that billions of people use trillions of times a day requires more nuance.

Our emoji have always favored expression over hyper-realism and in this new 3D world it means our designs can have dimension without being photorealistic. They need a pulse and a soul — not the cold precision of industrial CAD models. Have you looked closely at a real kangaroo? They’re terrifying 🙀. We don’t need anatomical perfection. With the power of illustration, we can capture the true, playful vibe of a kangaroo.

We also ran large-scale user studies to evaluate how changing emoji could potentially mess with human connection. The research unmasked universal truths: Users overwhelmingly prefer full-body animals over floating heads; adding props hurts comprehension; and tiny tweaks (like swapping the direction of a wink) can turn mild confusion into accidental outrage.

While our design process still begins with 2D drawings, it no longer ends there. In an industry first, Noto Emoji 3D is entirely available as true 3D models.

Moving from flat pixels to a three-dimensional world forced us to resolve architectural questions we’d never considered. What does the back of a smiley face look like? Is it a concave mask, a solid bouncy ball or a flat piece of paper?

Because language is meant to be shared, our entire Noto portfolio is completely open source. We’re handing over raw .OBJ files to the community so they can use them to build immersive VR worlds, indie apps or weird memes.

Color meets contrast

Emoji with the darkest skin tones can be difficult to see in dark mode — a problem for anyone who sends or receives them. That’s why we built an AI-powered contrast tool that analyzes each emoji at the pixel level, flags when the contrast ratio is too low and suggests high-contrast solutions that are implemented by designers 💪💪🏻💪🏼💪🏽💪🏾💪🏿.

Language is for the people

Now, Noto 3D emoji are yours. Remix them, break them and stretch them into shapes we never could have anticipated. Language only truly lives when we find new ways to use it. ⛲

entries.title: Experience the legacy of Estadio Azteca on Google Earth.
entries.summary: Relive 60 years of soccer history at Mexico City’s Aztec Stadium with Google Earth and the ICA Foundation.
entries.content:
Mexico City's Estadio Azteca made soccer history last month during the tournament opener between Mexico and South Africa. In hosting this match, it became the first stadium in the world to host games in three global soccer showdowns: 1970, 1986 and 2026.

And now you can experience the storied arena without being in the stands.

Through a Google Earth and Fundación ICA collaboration, you can get an up-close look at Estadio Azteca through an interactive digital canvas and imagery dating to the 1960s.

From its origins in 1961 to the clashes that crowned legends like Pelé and Maradona, the stadium has been home to six decades of sporting history that are now yours to explore. Experience the full story and take the field on Google Earth.

entries.title: Beach vibes and temporary wallpaper are trending for back-to-school season.
entries.summary: College students are eager to turn their dull dorm rooms into cozy spaces. Here are the Google Search trends we’re seeing.
entries.content:
As summer winds down, students will soon trade summer jobs for back-to-school shopping. In fact, Google Search interest for popular back-to-school items — from mini fridges to shower shoes and more — spikes annually around the first of August.

College students are eager to turn their dull dorm rooms into cozy, personalized spaces. Here’s what we’re seeing.

  • Students are being their own interior designers. “3D rendering dorm room” was a breakout search over the last month in the U.S. and “dorm designer” spiked +525%.
  • Popular design trends will bring a little bit of summer to the dorms. Dorm dwellers are looking for relaxing room vibes. Over the past month three of the top trending styles for dorm inspo are "coastal," “boho” and “beach,” and the top colors — pink, blue and green — give off sunset vibes.
  • Decor can add personality without permanent changes. “Dorm room wallpaper” is a breakout search over the past month; pink is the top color and hydrangea is the top pattern.
  • Students are looking for fun lighting and furniture. “Fairy lights” are the top trend for dorm lighting, and “desk hutch for dorm” is a top trending “... for dorm” search in the past month.

Check out our shopping blog for tips on how to use AI Search tools to help you shop this back-to-school season.
